meaning: n.拱形圆顶,圆屋顶adj.跳的,跳过的,夸大的
examples: Vaulting the stile, he headed diagonally across the paddock.
At the 1896 Olympics in Athens, a bamboo pole was used to set the vaulting record of 10 feet 6 inches.
Yet this vaulting ambition brought disaster.
We followed the Geyser River toward the vaulting spouts and red-yellow soils of the great Stained Glass geysers.
The sense of vaulting entitlement is gone, even if much of the squad is the same.
